item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Am in full support of these

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. So, we have a few tales based on this article, The Picture of an Unian and In The Eye Of The Beholders to be specific. Since tales can usually always use more attention working in some inline links out to them should not be too hard. Additionally, the mention of MTF Eta-10 can be in-line linked to their hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Figure 1: A photograph of SCP-3864 taken upon recovery. Taken 5 minutes prior to Figure 2.

To this:

Figure 1: A photograph of SCP-3864 taken upon recovery. Taken 5 minutes prior to Figure 2.

Changing this:

When a subject (henceforth referred to as SCP-3864-1) views SCP-3864 directly, within 15 minutes SCP-3864-1 will begin to gradually lose all pigmentation and most body heat. At this time, the likeness of SCP-3864-1 will begin to appear in SCP-3864. SCP-3864-1 will not be bothered by these processes. Within 30 minutes, SCP-3864-1 will become completely void of all pigmentation and have an average body temperature of 10° Celsius, as well as SCP-3864 showing a full likeness of SCP-3864-1. SCP-3864-1 will then die of severe dehydration and hypothermia. There is no recorded method of halting or slowing these processes.

To this:

When a subject (henceforth referred to as SCP-3864-1) views SCP-3864 directly, within 15 minutes SCP-3864-1 will begin to gradually lose all pigmentation and most body heat. At this time, the likeness of SCP-3864-1 will begin to appear in SCP-3864. SCP-3864-1 will not be bothered by these processes. Within 30 minutes, SCP-3864-1 will become completely void of all pigmentation and have an average body temperature of 10° Celsius, as well as SCP-3864 showing a full likeness of SCP-3864-1. SCP-3864-1 will then die of severe dehydration and hypothermia. There is no recorded method of halting or slowing these processes.

Changing this:

SCP-3864 was recovered on 5/18 by MTF Eta-10 from [REDACTED], Virginia. The Foundation became aware of SCP-3864’s existence when Foundation staff planted in local police units were notified of multiple missing persons reports supposedly linked to viewing a painting. Upon securing SCP-3864, members of MTF Eta-10 were able to confirm with neighboring residents that SCP-3864-1 was in fact the man who owned the house SCP-3864 was recovered from. All those who confirmed this later appeared in SCP-3864, having viewed it directly. A Class-C amnestic was administered to all neighboring residents to cover the disappearances.

To this:

SCP-3864 was recovered on 5/18 by MTF Eta-10 from [REDACTED], Virginia. The Foundation became aware of SCP-3864’s existence when Foundation staff planted in local police units were notified of multiple missing persons reports supposedly linked to viewing a painting. Upon securing SCP-3864, members of MTF Eta-10 were able to confirm with neighboring residents that SCP-3864-1 was in fact the man who owned the house SCP-3864 was recovered from. All those who confirmed this later appeared in SCP-3864, having viewed it directly. A Class-C amnestic was administered to all neighboring residents to cover the disappearances.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I joined the site, I was astonished when this article reached +20. I celebrated at +50 and was shell shocked at +75. Here we are at +102. Thank you to everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I haven't really read this yet, and I was actually blown away. Those interviews are some top-notch stuff.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why thank you, sir! I'm actually adding more tales and hopefully SCP formatted articles in the coming future, hoping to build a canon arc.

edit: that never happened lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In its original incarnation, the article ended on a relatively melodramatic love story. But the addendum and crosslink added new layers.

That is how you build a Tale sequel to an SCP article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New addendum to account for the tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: y'all gotta TELL me when gay shit gets posted. i never even saw this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll bite, because I'm a sucker for cute shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let! The! D-classes! Be happy!!

But for real though, now I'm kind of horrified to think about what happens to people who were trapped in the painting after someone else is trapped in it. Are they lost in a limbo or are they dead?

item.title: Late credits
item.content:encoded:
Yo this has been a while coming, but I forgot to credit people who helped me out with crit. So thanks to everyone on IRC and the forums.

Special thanks to Modern_Erasmus and 9Volt for the direct crit both on the forums and in IRC

Super special thanks to The Great Hippo for practically writing the Containment Procedures when they were cutting them down for me, and helping me learn to write them better.

Other critique thanks:
DrSevere
Leveritas
The_Raff
JackalRelated
Pedantique
Flawed
Cereza

Thanks to all else as well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ultimately I feel like I should downvote this. The weird tangent it goes off on narrative-wise leaves an unpleasant taste in my mouth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why thank you, sir! I do plan on adding more experiment logs in the future. Sort of like it actually happens lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, guess I didn't read carefully enough. Still though, I really enjoyed it and I cannot wait to see where the experiments go from here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who said tests were stopped completely? Only tests involving persons that are close or have had previous interaction with SCP-3864-1 were stopped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one, but I feel like it's a bit uncharacteristic of the foundation to stop experimenting on things unless they pose a serous threat to existence (and maybe even not then). I think this has awesome potential for more tests. For instance, what would happen in the case of:

  • Two people viewing the painting at once (or even more; a team of field agents, maybe?)
  • Someone equipped with tools to experiment with the painting world (Seems like the people keep their belongings when they get transported)
  • Painting additional objects into the painting
  • Someone with a paintbrush and paint entering the painting

Great work so far, really enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like the new pictures better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. Seemed more of a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much! And yeah, I kind of had that feeling too, but at the same time, I felt it gave a little mystery to the character. Almost a bit too…sophisticated sounding for the standard D-Class, kind of makes you wonder about the background.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Language is a tiny bit awkward in places (I have trouble believing a rough-around-the-edges D-Class would use the phrase "Bah, I feel no pity for him"), but otherwise this is great. Really nice cleanup of the earlier version, easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can respect that. Although, you do have to admit it was probably the easiest way to avoid any accidental copyright issues.

That being said, I'm definitely open to suggestions if you think there is a way I could improve the pictures, or even a good replacement for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, I can't get over the fact that those are photographs with a filter applied.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you thank you! Three weeks and 8 drafts haha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, this has certainly changed a ton from the original draft. +1 for a nice d-class love story!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aren't we? We are. lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my.

You and your house are absolutely fabulous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is kind of implied, actually, as in the beginning of the article it states that there has never been more than one subject inside of SCP-3864.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that picture is indeed me! I am a sexy motherfucker and decided to grace the foundation with my image

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A question that kept pounding my head since the first stage of your draft1: The guy in the pictures is you?

Footnotes
1. But i couldn't give critique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
cute, and tragic.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eyyyy you caved in

I think the sentences describing their actions (like S.A and S.B share a laugh) should be italized not bolded to make it look neater. Just a suggestion.

Also

I just love fucking cheeseburgers, man.

This sounds so wrong.

ETA: As far as I understand, the painting only record the last person to enter it, right? If so, the entrance of Subject B would make Subject A disappear. For more tragic value, stop all testing, thus leaving Subject B in eternal guilt and suffering.
